channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: England bowl first as they look to seal T20I series win over India LIVE!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Tinubu Seeks Senate Approval To Strengthen Senior Secondary Education Commission
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has transmitted the National Senior Secondary Education Commission (Amendment) Bill, 2026 to the Senate, seeking legislative approval to strengthen the administration and governance of public senior secondary education across Nigeria. The President’s request was conveyed in a l

item.title: Zambian vice president survives as helicopter crashes on takeoff
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Zambia goes to the polls on August 13 with President Hakainde Hichilema expected to win a second term as head of the country, one of Africa's top copper producers. The post Zambian vice president survives as helicopter crashes on takeoff appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Anambra ranks among top states in Open Government Healthy Status rating
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Anambra ranks among Nigeria's top-performing states with a 100% Healthy rating in the latest OGP sub-national assessment of governance reforms. The post Anambra ranks among top states in Open Government Healthy Status rating appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: “FCT Workers Hate Me” – Wike
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Minister, Nyesom Wike, says his insistence on redirecting government spending from official travels and conferences to infrastructure projects has cost him the goodwill of many civil servants. He declared that he would rather be criticised than approve what he conside

item.title: Political Assassination: Should We Take Peter Obi Seriously?
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Etim Etim  Murder for political reasons is not rare in Nigeria, so when Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) presidential candidate, Peter Obi, said the other day that he might be assassinated

item.title: Tinubu under pressure as fake Nigerian government agency sparks political storm
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: President ordered investigation after fictitious federal body allocated funding and office space, triggering renewed scrutiny of corruption A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3 billion naira (£705,248) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest demo

item.title: Tinubu transmits bill to establish criminal justice monitoring council to Senate
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The bill aims to ensure that Nigeria's criminal justice system promotes efficient management of criminal justice institutions and guarantees the speedy dispensation of justice. The post Tinubu transmits bill to establish criminal justice monitoring council to Senate appeared first on Premium Times N

item.title: EPL: Bruno Guimaraes agrees personal terms with Arsenal
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Bruno Guimaraes has reportedly agreed personal terms on a five-year contract with Arsenal. The post EPL: Bruno Guimaraes agrees personal terms with Arsenal appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Abia Govt pledges intervention as gully erosion devastates communities
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Abia State Government has said that it is working diligently to intervene in the plight of erosion-ravaged communities across the state. Commissioner for Environment, Mr. Philemon Asonye Ogbonna, gave the assurance to the indigenes of Amaewu Amamba Uzuakoli and Atta Emede Ibeku in Bende and Umuahia

item.title: UBEC writing new chapter in basic education, says Executive Secretary
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: UBEC says it recovered over N100bn in dormant education funds to improve classrooms, teacher development and digital learning nationwide. The post UBEC writing new chapter in basic education, says Executive Secretary appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NIS Directs Nigerians In Us To Apply Visa In Person, Ends OIS Role
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) has announced the immediate disengagement of Online Integrated Solutions (OIS Services) as the operator of Nigeria’s Visa Application and Submission Centres in the United States. The development was disclosed in a public notice published on the service&#82

item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: The Pan-African Payment and Settlement System (PAPSS) has added its most significant member yet. The Bank of Central…

item.title: Greek F-16 fighter jet crash-lands at busy Zakynthos airport
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The runway is closed after the jet reportedly landed without deploying its landing gear before bursting into flames.

item.title: FG expands Anti-Kidnap Fusion Cell to states, reviews security strategy
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Federal Government has reviewed its anti-kidnapping strategy and commenced the second phase of the expansion of the Multi-Agency Anti-Kidnap Fusion Cell to states. The move is part of efforts to strengthen intelligence sharing and improve operational coordination against kidnapping across the co

item.title: Micron’s stock surges on multibillion-dollar U.S. manufacturing push
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The memory-chip giant is reversing its recent slide after increasing its investment in the U.S. semiconductor supply chain.

item.title: Tambuwal to Tinubu: Put security before politics
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Aminu Tambuwal urges President Tinubu to prioritise security over politics during a visit to victims of bandit attacks in Sokoto. The post Tambuwal to Tinubu: Put security before politics appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Israeli military eliminates Hamas commander, Waheed Salam
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Israeli military on Thursday announced the elimination of Waheed Abu Salam, identified as a former commander of the Western Company in Khan Yunis. In a statement posted on X, the Israel Defense Forces, IDF, alleged that Abu Salam infiltrated Israel during the October 7 attack

item.title: Tinubu transmits bill to reform secondary education system to NASS
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The proposed reform is coming amid growing public debate over the federal government’s reported plan to abolish the separation between Junior Secondary School (JSS) and Senior Secondary School (SSS). The post Tinubu transmits bill to reform secondary education system to NASS appeared first on Premiu

item.title: Tech Drives US Stocks Higher as Traders Downplay Iran War Risks
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US stocks rose as investors put aside concerns over the latest flare-up between the US and Iran to pile on technology stocks after recent declines spurred dip buying.

item.title: Bitcoin just crossed 200 days of warning sign investors cannot ignore
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.description: In the final, Muchova will face Czech ninth seed Linda Noskova or Ukrainian 12th seed Marta Kostyuk, who meet in the other semi-final later on Thursday. The post Muchova Overpowers Ex-Grand Slam Champion Gauff In Thriller To Reach First Wimbledon Final appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Mali rebels attack convoy of Russian reinforcements headed to besieged military camp
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Tuareg separatists from the Azawad Liberation Front and their jihadist allies attacked a convoy of Russian soldiers and Malian troops Thursday as they were en route to reinforce a military camp in the northern town of Anefis.

item.title: Transfer: Bruno Guimaraes agrees deal with Arsenal
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Newcastle captain, Bruno Guimaraes, has agreed terms on a five-year contract with Arsenal. According to L’Equipe, Guimaraes remains adamant about joining the Premier League champions this summer. The Brazil international is now waiting on Newcastle to reach an agreement with Arsenal. The Gunne

item.title: MiniMax Seeks $1.9 Billion From Share Sale, Convertible Bond
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: MiniMax Group Inc. is looking to raise as much as HK$14.54 billion ($1.9 billion) from selling new shares and convertible bonds, according to terms of the deals seen by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Kanu Agabi, C’ River’s Priceless Gift to Nigeria, Gov Otu Hails Legal Luminary At 80
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Cross River State Governor, Senator Bassey Edet Otu, has eulogized one of Nigeria’s foremost legal icons, Chief Kanu Godwin Agabi (SAN), as the distinguished statesman clocks 80.  The governor described

item.title: Edo Security Corps arrest three over alleged murder, secret burial of girl
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Operatives of the Edo State Security Corps, ESSC, have arrested three suspects for alleged murder and secret burial of one Hannah, an indigene of Kebbi State. DAILY POST reports that the Deputy Corps Commander in Charge of Operations, Richard Balogun, disclosed this while parading the suspects in Be

item.title: Okpebholo seeks traditional rulers’ support to tackle insecurity
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Edo Governor Monday Okpebholo urges traditional rulers to support efforts against insecurity and criminal activities across the state. The post Okpebholo seeks traditional rulers’ support to tackle insecurity appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Adeleke alleged an ongoing coordinated and illegal operation by opposition elements to buy voters’ cards and extract their Personal Identification Numbers (PINs) to disenfranchise voters.

item.title: Global PC Market Shipments Drop 4.9% As Chip Shortage Threatens AI Boom
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The global personal computer (PC) market has recorded its first quarterly decline in more than two years as a worsening shortage of memory chips disrupted supply chains, pushing up prices and threatening the industry’s much-anticipated artificial intelligence (AI)-driven upgrade cycle. New fig

item.title: Court Adjourns Binance Tax Evasion Case Over Settlement Talks
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has adjourned the Federal Government’s alleged tax evasion case against cryptocurrency exchange Binance Holdings Ltd until September 24, 2026, to allow both parties more time to pursue an out-of-court settlement. Justice Emeka Nwite fixed the new date on Thursda

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: While some retailers still accept checks, an attempt to pay with anything other than cash or card is likely to result in a blank stare from a Gen Z cashier.

item.description: By Golok Nanmwa JOS — Jos Electricity Distribution Plc (JED) has raised concern over the rising incidence of electricity theft in Plateau State, disclosing that about 45 per cent of the energy supplied to the state is lost to illegal activities. item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Anthony Joshua wouldn't object if his blockbuster showdown with Tyson Fury has to start at 2am in order for it to be held in the UK.

item.title: Airbus Employees in Spain Go on Strike Over Working Conditions
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Airbus SE employees at a major manufacturing site in Spain have gone on strike in protest of what they say are deteriorating work conditions over the past years.

item.title: Trump Stops in UK to Switch Air Force One Planes
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description:
President Trump’s return from the NATO summit in Turkey took a detour when he switched Air Force One planes in the UK.
Annmarie Hordern explains why the new Qatari-donated jet is raising security questions. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State has disclosed that bandits abducted his brothers and demanded N300 million as ransom. Lawal, however, said he refused to pay the ransom so as not to encourage their activities. Speaking in Abuja on Thursday, Lawal reiterated that he will not negotiate with

item.description: The United States and Iran are continuing to trade strikes on July 9 for a second straight day. after President Donald Trump said the ceasefire between the two countries was "dead". item.title: Turkey: Erdogan gives NATO leaders pistols with ammunition after summit
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: NATO leaders received a vintage revolver, along with a box of ammunition, from Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan after Wednesday's summit in Ankara.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ikechukwu Nnochiri ABUJA — The Federal High Court in Abuja on Thursday adjourned until July 20 the trial of two alleged commanders of Ansaru, an Al-Qaeda-affiliated terrorist group. The defendants, Mahmud Usman, also known as Abu Bara’a, and Abubakar Abba, popularly called Mahmud Al-Nigeri

item.description: The First Lady should not be discouraged from well-doing. She’s in a different world from that of the Iya Alakara of old. This chill-less generation would not only welcome the Good Samaritan but also demand authenticity and scrutinise him. The feisty debate over First Lady Oluremi Tinubu’s comment o

item.title: Senate Passes Bill To Establish Agency For Management Of Recovered Assets
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Senate on Thursday passed two key judiciary-related bills, including a landmark legislation establishing an independent agency to recover, manage and dispose of assets linked to unlawful activities. The upper chamber approved the Legal Practitioners Act (Repeal and Re-enactment) Bill, 2026 (SB.

item.title: Schlage’s Sense Pro unlocks the door so I don’t have to
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The Schlage Sense Pro is a beautiful smart lock. Sleek, discreet, and simple to use, it's Schlage's smartest lock to date. Thanks to ultra-wideband (UWB), it unlocks as I walk up to my front door; I don't need to enter a code or tap my phone or press my finger against it. I've tested several [&#8230

item.title: US 30-Year Bond Auction Set to Draw Highest Yield in 20 Years
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: An auction of 30-year Treasury bonds Thursday is poised to draw the highest yield since at least 2006, underscoring how swelling bond supply is driving investors to demand higher returns from government debt.

item.title: Peller Recounts Ordeal After Release From Police Custody
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Popular Nigerian TikTok star, Habeeb Hamzat, better known as Peller has recounted his time in the police custody, revealing that he spent nearly four days in a cell without eating or bathing. New Telegraph had earlier reported that Peller was arraign before a Lagos High Court on Tuesday after he was

item.title: Oil Edges Lower as Traders Assess Renewed US-Iran Hostilities
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil edged lower as traders assessed renewed US strikes against Iran, with the limited scope of the hostilities prompting bets that energy shipments through the Persian Gulf will resume their recent recovery. Leslie Palti-Guzman, founder at Energy Vista, discusses the outlook for energy as tensions r

item.title: Flood claims one-year-old girl in Calabar
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ike Uchechukwu, Calabar A one-year-old girl has died after floodwaters triggered by a heavy downpour swept into her family’s apartment in Calabar, Cross River State. The rain, which began at about 2 a.m. on Thursday, inundated homes along Mayne Avenue, off Atu Street, in Calabar South Local

item.description: The deal comes as Africa's EV market grows but remains limited by two problems: financing and charging. Much of the region's electrification so far has centred on two- and three-wheelers.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Allied People’s Movement (APM) has challenged the All Progressives Congress (APC)-led Federal Government to account for the proceeds of fuel subsidy removal amid the increasing suffering and hunger in the country. The party recalled that President Bola Tinubu, upon inauguration in May 2023, anno

item.description: The Bola Tinubu administration signed an agreement with the World Bank for a $750 million loan, following the Bank’s approval on June 14, 2024, five days before the final disbursement deal was concluded with the Nigerian government. item.description: Syria regains voting rights in the OPCW as new leadership makes progress in addressing chemical weapons issues.

item.description: Stronger earnings and a broadening rally will power European stocks to new highs this year, according to UBS Group AG strategists, who are now the biggest bulls among forecasters tracked by Bloomberg.

item.description: The nostalgia is real! Netflix just dropped the trailer for Tyler Perry’s "Why Did I Get Married Again?" starring Taraji P. Henson, Jill Scott, and the original cast, premiering September 2026. The post Netflix Drops First Teaser for Tyler Perry’s “Why Did I Get Married Again?” Starring

item.title: EFCC arraigns Miyetti Allah leader Bodejo over alleged $2.33 million money laundering
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has arraigned the National President of Miyetti Allah Kautal Hore, Bello Abdullahi Bodejo, before the Federal High Court in Abuja over alleged money laundering involving $2.33 million. The post EFCC arraigns Miyetti Allah leader Bodejo over alleged

item.title: Tinubu Seeks Repeal Of ACJA 2015, Wants New Criminal Justice Law
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu, on Thursday, urged the Senate to repeal the Administration of Criminal Justice Act, ACJA, 2015 and enact the Administration of Criminal Justice Act, 2026. Tinubu’s request was contained in a letter addressed to the President of the Senate, Senator Godswill Akpabio and read at

item.title: Strait of Hormuz: What has happened since the US-Iran MoU on June 17?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: More than 500 ships have passed through the strait since June 17, but many remain stuck.

item.title: Arsenal sign former Leeds goalkeeper Illan Meslier on free transfer
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: Premier League champions Arsenal have signed former Leeds United goalkeeper Illan Meslier on a free transfer, the club confirmed in an official statement on Thursday. The post Arsenal sign former Leeds goalkeeper Illan Meslier on free transfer appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Xenophobia: Reps urge Nigerian govt to summon South African envoy over killing of citizens
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The lawmakers also rejected proposals to suspend diplomatic relations with Pretoria. The post Xenophobia: Reps urge Nigerian govt to summon South African envoy over killing of citizens appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Transfer: OGC Nice chief gives update on Moffi’s future
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: OGC Nice sporting director, Roger Ricort has said Terem Moffi will leave the club on a permanent basis this summer, DAILY POST reports. Moffi spent the second half of last season on loan at Portuguese Superliga champions FC Porto. The Nigeria international pushed to leave Nice after he was attacked

item.title: We Raised FCT Monthly IGR To Over N40bn In 3 Years – Wike
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Minister, Nyesom Wike, said the FCT Administration has increased its monthly internally generated revenue (IGR) from about N9 billion to more than N40 billion since he assumed office, attributing the surge to sweeping reforms in revenue collection and administration.
